name: Sarah Jenkins
clinical_note: Patient presents with severe acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain, starting 12 hours ago. Pain is sharp and has migrated from the periumbilical region. Associated with nausea, localized anorexia, and low-grade fever (38.1 C). On physical exam, positive McBurney's sign and rebound tenderness. Recommending abdominal ultrasound to confirm acute appendicitis.

name: Marcus Chen
clinical_note: Patient complains of chest tightness and pressure radiating down the left arm, onset during mild exertion 2 hours ago. History of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. ECG reveals ST-segment depression in anterior leads. Troponin levels elevated. Immediate referral to cardiology for urgent coronary angiography and potential percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) under primary diagnosis of non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI).

name: Elena Rostova
clinical_note: Patient presents with dry cough, shortness of breath, and fatigue for 3 days. History of mild intermittent asthma. SpO2 on room air is 96%. Wheezing noted bilaterally. Suspected viral bronchitis triggering acute asthma exacerbation. Prescribed albuterol nebulizer treatment and short course of oral prednisone.
